abstract: On February 6, 2023, Türkiye experienced one of the most devastating earthquake disasters in its history, significantly impacting ten provinces. This study evaluates the comprehensive response of the Ministry of National Education (MoNE) in the aftermath of the disaster, focusing on its dual role as a provider of humanitarian aid and a facilitator of educational continuity. Guided by international frameworks for education in emergencies, such as those established by INEE and UNESCO-IIEP, the MoNE leveraged its human resources and the production capacity of vocational institutions to mitigate the disaster's effects. Findings indicate that the MoNE served as a primary producer and distributor of food, heating materials, and shelter for affected citizens. Simultaneously, the Ministry prioritized the rapid resumption of schooling by establishing learning environments in tents, containers, and prefabricated buildings, while deploying volunteer teachers and psychological counselors to provide psychosocial support. Key policy actions included the creation of hospital classrooms for injured students, the facilitation of school transfers to other provinces, and the provision of free boarding and private education scholarships for earthquake victims. The study concludes that the MoNE effectively integrated its previous experience from the COVID-19 pandemic into its disaster response, successfully implementing evidence-based policies to ensure that education remained uninterrupted despite the extensive regional destruction.
